document.name: 21 Hungarian Kitchen
document.shortDescription: Modern Hungarian kitchen on Rózsadomb edge — tasting menus and wine pairings near the Castle.
document.longDescription:
21 Hungarian Kitchen on Fortuna utca serves contemporary Hungarian cuisine with cellar pairings on the Buda hill fringe. The intimate dining room suits anniversaries after castle sightseeing. Reserve on 21restaurant.hu.

Sources: https://www.21restaurant.hu/

document.name: Gellért Brasserie
document.shortDescription: Grand hotel dining at Gellért — brasserie menu and Sunday brunch at Szent Gellért tér.
document.longDescription:
Gellért Brasserie in the Danubius Hotel serves international and Hungarian dishes with spa-day guests and river views. Sunday brunch fills the art-nouveau dining room. Reservations on danubiusgellert.com.

Sources: https://www.danubiusgellert.com/dining

document.name: Buena Vista Bistro
document.shortDescription: Island-adjacent bistro near Óbuda shipyards — grills, burgers, and terrace beer by the Danube.
document.longDescription:
Buena Vista Bistro on Királyok útja serves casual grills and beer on a terrace facing the Óbuda Island bridges. Concert-goers stop here before Budapest Park shows. Menu on buenavista.hu.

Sources: https://www.buenavista.hu/

document.name: 3D Café & Bistro
document.shortDescription: Neighborhood bistro by Aquincum — daily menus and terrace seating on Szentendrei út.
document.longDescription:
3D Café & Bistro serves Hungarian home-style plates near the Aquincum ruins with a sunny terrace for museum visitors. The casual room bridges Roman Óbuda and modern residential streets. Hours on 3dcafe.hu.

document.name: Kacsa Étterem
document.shortDescription: Duck specialist on Fő utca — classic Hungarian waterfowl dishes near Bécsi út in Óbuda.
document.longDescription:
Kacsa Étterem on Fő utca is famed for duck liver and roast duck in a traditional Óbuda dining room steps from Bécsi út. Wine pairings highlight Hungarian whites. Reserve on kacsaetterem.hu.

document.shortDescription: Specialty roastery café by Deák — single-origin espresso and filter steps from the metro hub.
document.longDescription:
MADÁL on Madách Imre út roasts in-house and serves precise espresso and filter coffee near Deák Ferenc tér. The compact bar is a favorite for takeaway and quick sit-downs before exploring central Pest. Beans and hours are on madalcoffee.com.

document.name: Pierrot Café
document.shortDescription: Castle-side garden café on the Buda bank — coffee and cakes with Parliament views across the river.
document.longDescription:
Pierrot Café on Buda's Fő utca offers courtyard seating with postcard views of the Parliament building. Pastries, coffee, and light meals make it a calm stop on the Castle District side of the Danube. Hours and menus are listed on pierrot.hu.

Sources: https://www.pierrot.hu/

document.name: Szamos Café Vigadó
document.shortDescription: Classic Hungarian confectionery café by the Danube — cakes and coffee near Vigadó tér.
document.longDescription:
Szamos Café at Vigadó utca is part of the Szamos chocolate and cake dynasty with a river-close salon. Expect traditional tortes, espresso, and seasonal sweets on the promenade walk. Store hours are on szamos.hu.

Sources: https://www.szamos.hu/

document.name: Callas Café
document.shortDescription: Theatre-district café on Andrássy út — breakfast, cakes, and evening wine in an art-deco salon.
document.longDescription:
Callas Café occupies Andrássy út 20 with a ground-floor coffee bar and upstairs dining tied to the Callas House cultural space. It is a natural stop before Opera or Operetta shows. Hours and menus are on callascafe.hu.

Sources: https://www.callascafe.hu/

document.name: Opera Café Budapest
document.shortDescription: Modern café behind the Opera quarter — specialty coffee, brunch, and pre-show cakes on Hajós utca.
document.longDescription:

document.name: Daubner Cukrászda
document.shortDescription: Award-winning patisserie near Oktogon — cakes, coffee, and boxed desserts on Szilágyi fasor.
document.longDescription:
Daubner Cukrászda is a celebrated confectionery on Szilágyi Erzsébet fasor, a short tram ride from Oktogon. Locals queue for seasonal tortes, bonbons, and espresso in the salon. Opening hours and seasonal collections are on daubnercukraszda.hu.

Sources: https://www.daubnercukraszda.hu/

document.name: Espresso Embassy
document.shortDescription: Third-wave espresso bar near Király utca — single-origin shots and filter in a minimalist room.
document.longDescription:
Espresso Embassy on Arany János utca roasts specialty coffee a block from Király utca nightlife. Baristas serve precise espresso, flat whites, and pour-over with limited seating. Bean retail and hours are on espressoembassy.hu.

Sources: https://espressoembassy.hu/

document.name: Fröccsterasz
document.shortDescription: Spritz terrace on Liszt Ferenc tér — fröccs, coffee, and light bites on the famous dining square.
document.longDescription:
Fröccsterasz sits on Liszt Ferenc tér with outdoor tables built for Hungarian wine spritzers and casual plates. Evenings fill with pre-club crowds from nearby Király utca. Menu and hours on froccsterasz.hu.

document.name: Szpünik Café
document.shortDescription: Daytime café inside Szimpla — brunch and espresso before the ruin bar opens in the Jewish Quarter.
document.longDescription:
Szpünik Café operates daytime hours within the Szimpla Kert complex on Kazinczy utca with coffee, breakfast, and light lunch. It is a quieter entry point to the Jewish Quarter's ruin gardens. Details via szimpla.hu.

Sources: https://szimpla.hu/szpunik

document.name: Spíler Shanghai
document.shortDescription: Asian-fusion canteen in Gozsdu — noodles, brunch, and neon-lit dining in the courtyard passage.
document.longDescription:
Spíler Shanghai brings pan-Asian plates and weekend brunch to a Gozsdu Udvar unit on Király utca. The colorful room feeds passage strollers between ruin bars. Hours and menu on spilershanghai.hu.

Sources: https://spilershanghai.hu/

document.name: My Little Melbourne
document.shortDescription: Australian-style café off Kazinczy — flat whites, brunch, and filter coffee in the Jewish Quarter.
document.longDescription:
My Little Melbourne on Rumbach Sebestyén utca brings Aussie brunch culture a block from Kazinczy utca clubs. Expect avocado toast, quality espresso, and homemade cakes. Hours on mylittlemelbourne.hu.

document.name: Espresso Embassy Rákóczi

document.longDescription:
Espresso Embassy's Rákóczi út outpost continues the brand's specialty roasting with pour-over and espresso for commuters near Rákóczi tér. A compact bar fits quick morning stops. Hours on espressoembassy.hu.
